notes: South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation - Established in Dhaka on December 1985. Afghanistan became the newest member of SAARC at the 13th annual summit in 2005. Headquarters - Kathmandu, Nepal. Eight member States : Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka. Q.1776. What is the full form of ‘PM-SYM’? SSC CPO 27/06/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Pradhan Mantri Sanyukt Yojana Man-dhyan (b) Pradhan Mantri MukhiyaSukrasha Yojana (c) Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (d) Pradhan Mantri Shrestha Yojana Mulyankan Sol.1776.(c) Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-Dhan: It is a Central Sector Scheme announced in the Interim Budget of 2019. Nodal Ministry - Ministry of Labour and Employment. Aim - To provide social security and old age protection to unorganized workers in India. A minimum monthly pension of Rs 3,000 after the beneficiary turns 60 years old. Q.1777. The CPWD along with the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways works for public infrastructure. What is the full form of CPWD? Higher Secondary 26/06/2024 (Shift - 2) (a) Central Public Welfare Department (b) Central Public Welfare Design (c) Central Political Welfare Design (d) Central Public Works Department Sol.1777.(d) The Central Public Works Department (CPWD) operates under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs of India. It acts as the builder, developer, and manager of Central government properties. Over time, its operations have expanded to include roadways and bridge engineering. Established in 1854, its headquarters is located in the Central Secretariat in New Delhi.

Rural Infrastructure Development Fund created in 1995-96 under National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) with an initial corpus of Rs.2,000 crore. There are 39 eligible activities under RIDF. Eligible activities are classified under three broad categories: Agriculture and related sector, Social sector, Rural connectivity.
Intensive Agricultural District Programme (Launched in 1960) - Also known as a “package program”; Aim - To provide loans for seeds and fertilisers to farmers.

Fiscal. The FRBM Act (Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act), enacted in August 2003. Aim - To promote fiscal discipline, transparency, and accountability in the management of India's finances.

National Anti-Doping Agency - It is India's national organisation responsible for promoting, coordinating, and monitoring the doping control program in sports in all its forms. Established - November 2005. Headquarters - New Delhi.

Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups. It is a sub-classification of a Scheduled Tribe or section of a Scheduled Tribe that is considered more vulnerable than a regular Scheduled Tribe. PVTGs reside in 18 States and one union territory.

World Anti-Doping Agency - It was established in as an international independent agency composed and funded equally by the sport movement and governments of the world. The First World Conference on Doping in Sport held in Lausanne (Switzerland) in 1999. Headquarter - Montreal, Canada.

National Crime Records Bureau is an Indian government agency responsible for collecting and analyzing crime data as defined by the Indian Penal Code (IPC) and Special and Local Laws (SLL). Headquarters - New Delhi. Ministry - Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA). Established - 1986. It implementes Crime and Criminal Tracking Network System (CCTNS).
Report Program Generator. It is a high-level programming language for business applications, introduced in 1959 for the IBM 140. Some Major High Language - Python, Java, C++, JavaScript and C#.

The Recommended Dietary Allowance (RDA) is the average daily dietary intake level that is sufficient to meet the nutrient requirement of nearly all (97 to 98 percent) healthy individuals in a particular gender and life stage group.

Financial. IFSC stands for Indian Financial system Code. It is an 11 digit alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify bank branches within the National Electronic Funds transfer (NEFT) network by the central bank. Religious Places Q.1788. At which place was the magnificent temple of Saiva constructed by the great Rashtrakuta King ‘Krishna I’? SSC CHSL 02/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Badami (b) Kanchi (c) Kannauj (d) Ellora Sol.1788.(d) Ellora. The temples at Ellora Caves (Maharashtra) were built by the Rashtrakuta and Yadava dynasties. Rashtrakuta dynasty built the Hindu and Buddhist caves during their reign. The Kailasa Temple (dedicated to Lord Shiva) was built by Krishna I of the Rashtrakuta dynasty. It is the largest single-rock excavation in the world. Yadava dynasty built many of the Jain caves. The Ellora Caves are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The caves include 12 Buddhist caves, 17 Hindu caves, and 5 Jain caves. Q.1789. Brihadiswara Temple which exhibits Dravidian temple architecture is located in__________. SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) Dharmapuri (b) Thiruvananthapuram (c) Madurai (d) Thanjavur Sol.1789.(d) Thanjavur (Tamil Nadu). Brihadeshwara Temple (also known as Thanjai Periya Kovil and Rajarajeswaram) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. It was built by Tamil King Raja Raja Chola I. The temple is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site known as the “Great Living Chola Temples”, with the other two being Gangaikonda Cholapuram and Airavatesvara temple.

Eastern Ganga Dynasty. Konark Sun Temple is a 13th-century CE Sun temple at Konark in Puri district, Odisha. This temple is attributed to King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty.Temple is a classic illustration of Great Kalinga Architecture. It is dedicated to the God Surya. The famous Chandrabhaga Mela takes place here every year in the month of February. This temple is also called "Black Pagoda". The temple was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1984. Q.1791. Lad Khan Temple at Aihole in Karnataka is an example of which of the following styles of temple architecture? Higher Secondary 25/06/2024 (Shift - 4) (a) Dravida (b) Nagara (c) Vesara (d) Pagoda Sol.1791.(c) Vesara style of temples is a combination of both Nagara (North India) and Dravida (South India), its superstructure is bell-shaped. Examples of Vesara Style temples in Karnataka: Mahadeva temple (Ittagi); Kasivisvesvara temple (Lakkundi); Kallesvara Temple (Kukkanur).

Balaji Baji Rao. Trimbakeshwar Temple, a renowned religious center, houses one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and is located at the foot of Brahmagiri Hill in the city of Trimbak. Other notable temples and their builders include the Brihadeeswara Temple (Raja Raja Chola I of the Chola dynasty), Konark Sun Temple (King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga Dynasty), and the Kailasa Temple at Ellora (Krishna I of the Rashtrakuta dynasty).

Kamakhya Temple - Assam. Other temples and locations : Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga - Madhya Pradesh, Somnath Jyotirlinga Temple - Gujarat, Jagannath Temple - Odisha, Badrinath Temple - Uttarakhand, Golden Temple - Punjab, Meenakshi Temple - Tamil Nadu. Hoysaleswara Temple - Karnataka.

Uttar Pradesh. The Victory of Skandagupta over the Huns is mentioned in Bhitari Inscription. Other Religious Sites of Uttar Pradesh: Sarnath, Kashi Vishwanath Temple, Banke Bihari Temple, ISKCON Vrindavan, Banaras Ghats.

Bodh Gaya (Bihar). The Mahabodhi Temple (located on the banks of the Niranjana River) is one of the four holy sites related to the life of the Lord Buddha, and particularly to the attainment of Enlightenment. First temple was established in the 3rd century BC by Mauryan emperor Ashoka. Famous Temples in Bihar: Vishnupad Mandir and Mangala Gowri Temple (Gaya), Pawapuri Jal Mandir (Nalanda) and Mithila Shakti Peeth (Darbhanga).

Gupta Period. Bhitargaon Temple: It is situated in Kanpur (Uttar Pradesh) and is one of the earliest surviving brick temples of India. It was built during the 5th century AD. Other Gupta period Architectures: Ajanta, Ellora, Sarnath. Temples built during Gupta Period: Dashavatara Temple (Deogarh), Bhitari Temple (Ghazipur).
Odisha. Temples in Odisha: Jagannath Temple (Puri), Sun Temple (Konark), Maa Tara Tarini Temple (Purusottampur), Lingaraj Temple (Bhubaneswar), Brahmeswara Temple (Bhubaneswar).

Shravanabelagola (Hassan district of Karnataka) - Gomateshwara statue located in this place and built under the Ganga dynasty. Karuppanna Swamy Temple (Keelakuilkudi, Madurai), Shantinatha Basadi temple (Jinanathapura), Puliyarmala Jain Temple (Kalpetta Wayanad - Kerala). Q.1799. The famous 'Pashupatinath Temple' is located in which country from the following? SSC MTS 08/09/2023 (1st Shift) (a) Nepal (b) Bhutan (c) Maldives (d) Sri Lanka Sol.1799.(a) Nepal. Pashupatinath Temple (Dedicated to Lord Shiva): Stretches on both sides of the Bagmati River. It was conferred the status of a World Heritage Site in 1979 by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO).
Chhattisgarh. Temples in Chhattisgarh: Maa Bamleshwari Devi Temple, Mahamaya Temple, Chandrahasini Devi temple, BhoramdeoTemple, Danteshwari Temple.

Gopuram - A monumental entrance tower, built in the Dravidian Style. Tallest Gopuram - Ranganathaswamy Temple (Tamil Nadu). Southern Indian temples : Shikhara - crowning element at the top of the temple. Vimana - shape of the main temple tower. Q.1802. Builders of which of the following temples had constructed a 4 km long incline for placing 90 tonnes of stone on top of the tallest shikhara amongst temples of its time? Graduate Level 05/08/2022 (Shift - 3) (a) The Sun Temple (b) Rajarajeshvara Temple (c) Kandhariya Mahadeva Temple (d) Gangadikondha Temple Sol.1802.(b) Rajarajeshvara Temple. It was built by Rajaraja Chola I, in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to Lord Shiva.

Odissi. Lord Jagannath is worshipped as Vishnu or Narayana or Krishna and deity Balabhadra as Shesha. Q.1804. In which of the following countries is the Hindu religious place 'Chandranath Hill' located? Graduate Level 30/06/2023 (Shift - 1) (a) Bhutan (b) Nepal (c) Bangladesh (d) Sri Lanka Sol.1804.(c) Bangladesh. Chandranath Temple :- It is located on top of the Chandranath Hill, is a famous Shakti Peeth located near Sitakunda in Bangladesh. Q.1805. In North Indian temple architecture , which element denotes the superstructure or tower above the sanctum and the pillared mandapas? Matric Level 28/06/2023 (Shift - 1) (a) Kalasha (b) Amalaka (c) Shikhara (d) Antarala Sol.1805.(c) Shikhara - A tower, in elevation, which is progressively inclined in a convex curve. The three most common temple architecture are: Nagara in the north (between the Himalayas and Vindhyas), and Dravidian in the south. The Vesara style of temples is an admixture of Nagara and Dravidian styles.

Sri Jagannath Temple in Odisha. It was built by Anantavarman Chodaganga Deva In Kalinga - Style architecture. The temple was called the “White Pagoda” and is a part of Char Dham pilgrimages (Badrinath, Dwaraka, Puri, Rameswaram).

Lord Shiva. Ramappa Temple (Rudreshwara Temple or Ramalingeswara Temple) - Situated in Palampet in (Warangal) Telangana. It is a masterpiece of the Kakatiyan style of temple architecture. In July 2021, Ramappa Temple was declared as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Other famous temples (Telangana) - Chilkur Balaji Temple, Sanghi Temple, Gnana Saraswathi Temple, Surendrapuri Temple, Karmanghat Hanuman Temple, Beechupally Anjaneya Swamy Temple.
Jain. Dilwara Temple (Nagara Style of Architecture): Built by Vimal Shah and designed by Vastupala. Other Jain Architecture: Ellora Caves (Maharashtra), Hathi Gumpha.

Garbhagriha - Inner chamber of the temple where the main deity was placed. It is also known as Sanctum Sanctorium. Other parts of Hindu Temple : Mandapa - Incorporates space for a large number of worshippers, Shikhar - Mountain-like spire, Vahan - Vehicle of the temple’s main deity, Amalak and Kalash - Crowning elements. Q.1810. The Virupaksha Temple is situated at _______. SSC MTS 16/06/2023 (Evening) (a) Surat (b) Sanchi (c) Hampi (d)Jaipur Sol.1810.(c) Hampi. Virupaksha Temple (Pampapathi Temple) was built by Lakhan Dandesha in Dravidian style on the south bank of the river Tungabhadra at Hampi (Capital of Vijayanagara). Dedicated to Lord Siva. It is designated as a UNESCO World Heritage site (1986). Other Monuments at Hampi - Vittala Temple, Ganigatti Jain temple, Krishna Temple and Lotus Mahal.

Odisha (Bhubaneswar): Dedicated to Lord Shiva. Karnataka: The Chalukya Shiva Temple (LadKhan Temple) in Aihole. Maharashtra: Ter Temple, is a temple of Vamana (an incarnation of Vishnu), Trimbakeshwar Temple of Lord Shiva in Nashik. Tamil Nadu: Murugan Temple at Saluvankuppam, Brihadeeswarar Temple (Thanjavur), Iravatanesvara temple.
The Shore Temple (Dravidian style). It is located in Mahabalipuram (Tamil Nadu). It was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1984. It was built in the reign of Narasimhavarman II.

Carved cow representing South direction. The top of the column—the capital—has three parts. First, a base of a lotus flower (ubiquitous symbol of Buddhism). Then, a drum on which four animals are carved represents the four cardinal directions: a horse (west), an ox (east), an elephant (south), and a lion (north). Four lions stand at top of the drum, each facing in the four cardinal directions. Q.1814. In the Kandariya Mahadeva Temple, _______ was the place of ritual worship, where only the king, his family members and the priests used to gather. SSC CPO 10/11/2022 (Evening) (a) sanctum sanctorum (b) Mahamandap (c) antraal (d) Amalak Sol.1814.(a) sanctum sanctorum. Garbha griha (Temple's innermost part where the Murti of the deity is kept). Temple Other Part - Mahamaṇḍapa (greater hall). Antarala (small antechamber or foyer between garbhagriha and mandapa). Amalaka (notched stone disk, usually with ridges on the rim, that sits on the top temple's shikhara. Kandariya Mahadeva temple (Nagara Style of Architecture) (Madhya Pradesh) was constructed by king Dhaṇgadeva (Chandela dynasty) in 999 CE.

Tamil Nadu . Other famous temples of Tamil Nadu: Meenakshi Temple, Rameshwaram Temple, Kumari Amman Temple, Nagannathaswamy Temple, Brihadeeswarar Temple, Kapaleeshwarar Temple, Nataraja Temple, Arunachaleswarar Temple, Ekambareswarar, Mahabalipuram Shore, etc.
Himachal Pradesh. It is situated on the left bank of River Bhaga. It was renovated in 1912 CE by Lama Norbu Rinpoche.

‘Amalaka’ is one of the important elements of Temple Shikhara (Tower) in Nagara Shaili usually found in Uttara Bharatha. A mandir ‘kalash’ is a metal or stone spire used to top the domes of Hindu temples. In Hindu temple architecture, the ‘jagati' is the raised surface of the platform or terrace upon which some Buddhist or Hindu temples are built.

Nagara. The Khajuraho Group of Monuments is a group of Hindu temples and Jain temples in Chhatarpur district, Madhya Pradesh, India. They are a UNESCO World Heritage Site (1986). Q.1819. Madan Mohan temple is a centrally protected monument. It is situated in which state of India? SSC MTS 20/07/2022 (Morning) (a) Himachal Pradesh (b) Uttarakhand (c) Uttar Pradesh (d) Karnataka Sol.1819.(c) Uttar Pradesh (in Vrindavan). Madan Mohan temple was built by Sanatana Gosvami in 1580 AD. Some other famous temples of Uttar Pradesh are Ram Janmabhoomi Temple, Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi Mandir, Gorakhnath Math Temple, Kashi Vishwanath Temple, Banke Bihari Temple, Vrindavan, Sarnath temple, etc.

Features of Nagara style temple: It is built on a stone platform with steps leading up to it, It does not usually have elaborate boundary walls or gateways, Amalaka or Kalash which is installed on Shikhara, etc. Sub divisions : Rekha-Prasad or Latina, Shekari, Bhumija, Valabhi , and Phamsana.

Amritsar, Punjab. Golden Temple (Sri Harmandar Sahib) is the preeminent spiritual site of Sikhism. Some other spiritual sites of Sikhism are the Gurdwara Darbar Sahib Kartarpur in Kartarpur (Pakistan), and Gurdwara Janam Asthan in Nankana Sahib (Pakistan), etc.

Elephanta Caves. The sculpture represents three aspects of Shiva: identified as the Creator, the Preserver, and the Destroyer, respectively, along with Aghora or Bhairava (left half), Taptapurusha or Mahadeva (central full face), and Vamadeva or Uma (right half). Q.1823. Who established the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON)? SSC MTS 14/10/2021 (Morning) (a) Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada (b) Sri Chaitanya (c) Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati (d) Sri Ramakrishna Sol.1823.(a) Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. ISKCON was founded in in New York City by A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. Its core beliefs are based on Hindu scriptures, particularly the Bhagavad Gita and the Bhagavata Purana, and the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition.

Sikkim. Phodong Monastery (or Phodang) is a Buddhist monastery. Hemis Monastery is situated 45 Kms away from Leh in Ladakh. Q.1825. Shaikh Muinuddin Sijzi (Moinuddin Chishti) dargah is situated in which of the following cities? SSC CHSL 15/04/2021 (Morning) (a) Jalandhar (b) Ajmer (c) Panipat (d) Patna Sol.1825.(b) Ajmer. Dargah Sharif of Ajmer Sharif is a Sufi tomb (dargah) of the revered Sufi saint, Moinuddin Chishti, located at Ajmer, Rajasthan. The shrine has Chishti's grave (Maqbara).
Kerala. The temple is dedicated to Lord Ayyappa Swamy. Kerala's renowned Sabarimala Temple is to be renamed Sabarimala Sree Ayyappa Swamy Temple.
Gaur, Malda (West Bengal). Kadam Rasool Mosque was built by Sultan Nasseruddin Nusrat Shah in the year 1530-31.
Puri (Orissa). Govardhan Math or Govardhan Peetham is one amongst the four cardinal peethams established by the 8th century philosopher-saint Adi Shankara to preserve and propagate Sanatana Dharma.

Dashavatara Temple. Panchayatana is an architectural style where the main shrine is built on a rectangular plinth with four smaller subsidiary shrines at the four corners, making it a total of five shrines.
West Bengal. Terracotta temples are known for their Hindu temples made from local terracotta, such as the pyramid-shaped Rasmancha, established around 1600.

New Delhi. Swaminarayan Akshardham is the world's largest comprehensive Hindu temple. It measures 356 ft (109 m) in length, 141 ft high and ft in width. It was inaugurated on 6 November 2005. Q.1832. Kartarpur Sahib corridor connects Dera Baba Nanak Shrine in Gurdaspur, India to Kartarpur Gurudwara in Pakistan, which is located near the banks of the river _____. SSC CHSL 19/10/2020 (Morning) (a) Ravi (b) Jhelum (c) Sutlej (d) Chenab Sol.1832.(a) Ravi. Kartarpur Corridor was first proposed in early 1999 by Atal Bihari Vajpayee and Nawaz Sharif, the Prime Ministers of India and Pakistan respectively at that time. Q.1833. Birthplace of Guru Nanak, known as Nankana Sahib today, was earlier called. SSC CHSL 16/10/2020 (Morning) (a) Roda (b) Umarkot (c) Rai Bhoi di Talwandi (d) Luni Sol.1833.(c) Rai Bhoi di Talvandi. Guru Nanak was the founder of the Sikh religion and was born on 15th April 1469 in Pakistan.
Odisha.
Draupadi Ratha.

Jataka tales. These are Buddhist legends describing the previous births of the Buddha. These fables embed ancient morals and cultural lores that are also found in the fables and legends of Hindu and Jain texts. Awards Q.1837. Identify the correct statement with reference to the Padma awards. SSC MTS 22/10/2024 (1st Shift) (a) The Padma award can be used as a suffix or prefix to the awardees’ name (b) The total number of Padma awards to be given in a year should not more than 150. (c) The Padma Awards are given annually. (d) The names of the awardees are never published in the Gazette of India. Sol.1837.(c) Padma Awards, which were instituted in the year 1954, are announced every year on the occasion of Republic Day. The award is given in 3 categories, namely, Padma Vibhushan for exceptional and distinguished service; Padma Bhushan for distinguished service of a high order; and Padma Shri for distinguished service. The total number of awards to be given in a year (excluding posthumous awards and to NRI/foreigners/OCIs) should not be more than 120. The award does not amount to a title and cannot be used as a suffix or prefix to the awardees’ name. Q.1838. Which television channel presents the IMMIES Awards every year with an aim to reward the best talent in the Indian music industry? SSC CGL 26/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) B4U Music (b) MTV (c) Channel V (d) 9XM Sol.1838.(b) MTV. The MTV Immies are held every year with an aim to award the best talents in the Indian music industry. The Mirchi Music Awards, or IMMIES, are presented annually by Radio Mirchi to honor the best in Hindi film music. The first ceremony took place on March 28, 2009, at Taj Lands End, Mumbai, hosted by Shaan and Tulip Joshi. Q.1839. Norman Ernest Borlaug was awarded the _____ in for his contributions to the green revolution. SSC CHSL 03/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) Nobel Prize in Physics (b) Nobel Peace Prize (c) Nobel Prize in Chemistry (d) Nobel Prize in Physiology Sol.1839.(b) Nobel Peace Prize. Norman Borlaug was a renowned American agronomist and humanitarian, often referred to as the Father of the Green Revolution. Norman E Borlaug Award : recognizes exceptional scientists under 40 and someone who works in the field of food and nutrition security and hunger eradication. It is given in the honor of Norman Borlaug. Q.1840. In which year was the first Arjuna Award given to a woman hockey player? SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) 1963 (b) 1964 (c) 1962 (d) 1961 Sol.1840.(d) 1961. Arjuna Award: Awarded for consistent good performance in sports over 4 years. The winners of the Arjuna Award receive a statuette of Arjuna, a certificate and a cash prize 15 lakhs. First woman hockey player awarded - Anne Lumsden. Football Olympian PK Banerjee was the first footballer to win the prestigious accolade.

2009. The Padma Shri, is the fourth-highest civilian award of the Republic of India, after the Bharat Ratna, the Padma Vibhushan and the Padma Bhushan. Established - 1954. Mahendra Singh Dhoni: Awards - Padma bhushan (2018), Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award (2007).

Dhyan Chand Award. It is awarded annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Instituted in 2002. The first recipients - Shahuraj Birajdar (Boxing), Ashok Diwan (Hockey), and Aparna Ghosh (Basketball). Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna (previous name - Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award): Instituted in 1991-1992.

Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puruskar. An honour given by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. The award was launched in 2009. Awards and their cash prize: Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna - 25 lakh; Dronacharya Award Regular -10 lakh; Dronacharya Award Lifetime - 15 lakh; Arjuna Award - 15 lakh. Q.1844. In which year was the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna award renamed as Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna award, which is the highest sporting honour in the nation? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) 1991 (b) 2019 (c) 2021 (d) 2014 Sol.1844.(c) 2021. Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award : It is awarded annually by the “Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports”. The award comprises a medallion, a certificate, and a cash prize of Rs. 25 lakh. First recipient (1991-92) - Viswanathan Anand (Chess). Rani Rampal is the first woman Hockey Player to receive the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award in 2020. Winner (2022) - Achanta Sharath Kamal (Table Tennis). Q.1845. The government of India recently changed the name of Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award to Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award, Dhyan Chand served in the Indian army as a : SSC CPO 28/06/2024 (1st shift) (a) Major General (b) Major (c) General (d) Field Marshal Sol.1845.(b) Major. The Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award is India's highest sporting honor, awarded annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Dhyan Chand, a legendary hockey player, received the Padma Bhushan, India's third highest civilian award, in 1956. His birthday, August 29, is celebrated as National Sports Day. The Khel Ratna award comes with a prize of ₹25 lakh, recognizing outstanding achievements in sports.

2006. Prof M Yunus won the Nobel Peace Prize for founding the Grameen Bank and pioneering the concepts of microcredit and microfinance. The Prathama Bank of Moradabad, Uttar Pradesh, was the first Regional Rural Bank established on 2 October 1975.

C Subramaniam. He received Bharat Ratna award in 1998. The first Bharat Ratna was given to the three members in 1954: Shri Chakravarti Rajagopalachari, Dr. Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan, Dr. Chandrasekhara Venkata Raman. Other awardee: GovindBallabh Pant (1957), Pandurang Vaman Kane (1963), Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam (1997).

Arjuna Awards : Instituted in 1961; Cash prize - ₹15 lakh; First awarded to football Olympian PK Banerjee; First woman awardee - Anna Lumsden (Hockey). Arjuna Awards 2023 : Aditi Gopichand Swami (archery), Mohammed Shami (cricket), Aishwary Pratap Singh Tomar (shooting), among others. MAKA (The Maulana Abul Kalam Azad) Trophy : Given to an institution or university for top performance in inter-university tournaments. Dronacharya Award : India's highest sports honor for coaches. Rashtriya Khel Protsahan Puraskar : Awarded to organizations and individuals for sports promotion and development. Q.1849. Which Indian award was established in the year 1982, to promote and encourage scientific efforts in the field of environment and ecology ? Higher Secondary 20/06/2024 (Shift - 2) (a) Pitambar Pant National Environment Fellowship Award (b) Sanjay Gandhi Award for Environment and Ecology (c) B P Pal National Environment Fellowship Award for Biodiversity (d) Indira Gandhi Paryavaran Puraskar Sol.1849.(b) The Sanjay Gandhi Award for Environment and Ecology is awarded annually to an individual citizen of India engaged in scientific work in the field. It includes a cash prize of ₹100,000, with the announcement made in May or June of the following year. Eligibility is open to all Indian citizens.
Kamleshwar. He was awarded the Sahitya Akademi Award. Other novels: “Pati Patni Aur Woh”,, “Samagra Upanyas”.

1985. Dronacharya Award: The award comprises a bronze statuette of Dronacharya, a certificate, ceremonial dress, and a money prize of Rs 15 lakh (lifetime) and 10 Lakh (regular). The first recipients of the award were Bhalchandra Bhaskar Bhagwat (Wrestling), Om Prakash Bhardwaj (Boxing), and O. M. Nambiar (Athletics), who were honored in 1985.

1982. Kapil Dev (The Haryana Hurricane) : He was the captain of the Indian cricket team when India lifted a World Cup trophy (1983). Awards - Padma Bhushan (1991), Arjuna Award for Cricket (1979), C. K. Nayudu Lifetime Achievement Award (2013).

Jacobus Henricus van't Hoff. Nobel Prize - 1902 - Hermann Emil Fischer “in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered by his work on sugar and purine synthesis’”. - Svante August Arrhenius “in recognition of the extraordinary services he has rendered to the advancement of chemistry by his electrolytic theory of dissociation”. 1906 - Henri Moissan “in recognition of the great services rendered by him in his investigation and isolation of the element fluorine, and for the adoption in the service of science of the electric furnace called after him”.

1954. Linus Carl Pauling was also awarded with the Nobel Peace Prize (1962). Some Scientists who won Nobel Prize in Chemistry :- Otto Paul Hermann Diels and Kurt Alder (1950) - “for their discovery and development of the diene synthesis”. Sir Cyril Norman Hinshelwood and Nikolay Semenov (1956) - “for their research into the mechanism of chemical reactions”. Frederick Sanger (1958)- “for his work on the structure of proteins, especially that of insulin”.

Discovery of transistor effect. Nobel Prize: Started in 1901, Alfred Nobel (discovered the Dynamite in 1867). Indian who recieved Nobel prize: First - Rabindra Nath Tagore - 1913 (Literature), Chandrashekhara Venkat Raman - 1930 (Physics), Har Gobind Khorana - 1968 (Physiology or Medicine), Amartya sen - 1998 (Economics), Kailash Satyarthi - 2014 (Peace).

Republic Day. Post -independence, the first three gallantry awards were Param Vir Chakra, Maha Vir Chakra, and Vir Chakra, instituted on 26th January (effective from 15th August 1947). Later, Ashoka Chakra Class-I, Class-II, and Class-III were instituted on 4th January 1952, renamed as Ashoka Chakra, Kirti Chakra, and Shaurya Chakra in January 1967. Order of precedence is Param Vir Chakra, Ashoka Chakra, Mahavir Chakra, Kirti Chakra, Vir Chakra, and Shaurya Chakra.

Rabindranath Tagore :- Mystic, artist and Bengali polymath (great poet, philosopher, music composer and a leader of Brahma Samaj). Books written by Rabindranath Tagore - Gora (Fair-Faced) and Ghare -Baire (The Home and the World). National anthem of India composed by Rabindranath Tagore.

Muhammad Yunus. He is a Bangladeshi social entrepreneur, banker, economist and civil society leader who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize 2006 for founding the Grameen Bank and work to “create economic and social development from below”.

Charles Wilson. Frederick Soddy - A British chemist, won the 1921 Nobel Prize in chemistry for his research on radioactive decay and his formulation of the concept of isotopes. Ernest Lawrence - Nobel Prize in Physics in 1939 for his invention of the cyclotron. Harold Urey - Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1934 "for his discovery of deuterium, a heavy isotope of hydrogen".

Nelson Mandela and Frederik Willem de Klerk received the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993. Mahatma Gandhi (Symbol of non violence): Nominated for Nobel Peace Prize in 1937, 1938, 1939, 1947 and 1948 but never awarded the prize. Albert John Lutuli: Awarded the 1960 Nobel Peace Prize for his role in leading the non-violent anti -apartheid movement.

Kailash Satyarthi. The Nobel Peace Prize 2014 was awarded jointly to Kailash Satyarthi and Malala Yousafzai "for their struggle against the suppression of children and young people and for the right of all children to education".

Hansa Sharma. The Dronacharya award contains a medallion, a certificate, and a money prize of Rs 15 lakh (lifetime) and 10 Lakh (regular). First awarded (1985) - Bhalachandra Bhaskar Bhagwat (Wrestling), Om Prakash Bhardwaj (Boxing), O. M. Nambiar (Athletics).

Hemant Karkare was the chief of the Mumbai Anti-Terrorist Squad (ATS). He was killed in action during the 2008 Mumbai attacks. In 2009, he was posthumously given the Ashoka Chakra, India's highest peacetime gallantry decoration.
A.R. Rahman. Padma Shri (2000), Padma Bhushan (2010), two Grammy Awards (both in 2010).
U.S.A. Bessie Awards (The New York Dance and Performance Awards; Established in 1983) are awarded annually to dance artists presenting their work in New York City.
Vyjayanthimala. Award: Padma Vibhushan (2024).
National Film Award. The most prominent film award ceremony in India. Established in 1954. It has been administered by the Indian government's Directorate of Film Festivals since 1973.

Jean Baptiste Perrin. Charles Thomson Rees Wilson (1927; for his invention of the cloud chamber). Murray Gell- Mann (1969; for the classification of elementary particles and their interactions). Owen Willans Richardson (1928; for his work on the thermionic phenomenon and especially for the discovery of the law named after him). Q.1869. In which year was the International Booker Prize established? SSC CHSL 10/06/2022 (Evening) (a) 2005 (b) 1969 (c) 1981 (d) 1918 Sol.1869.(a) 2005. 2022- Geetanjali Shree (Tomb of Sand), 2021 - David Diop (At Night All Blood Is Black).
KS Krishnan. Dr Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar award: The purpose of the award is to recognise outstanding Indian work in science and technology.
Medicine. The Bidhan Chandra Roy Award was instituted in 1962. Legal Era Awards given in the field of law. The Pritzker Prize award is given in the Architecture field.

France. The Gitanjali Literary prize has two aims: to contribute to the promotion of the French language in India and also to the promotion of Indian works in France. Q.1873. The 'Amrita Devi Bishnoi National Award' is given for : SSC MTS 12/10/2021 (Morning) (a) environment protection (b) energy protection (c) wildlife protection (d) biodiversity protection Sol.1873.(c) wildlife protection. The 'Amrita Devi Bishnoi National Award ' is in remembrance of Amrita Devi Bishnoi, who died in the 1730 Khejarli massacre while trying to protect a grove of Khejri trees in Khejarli, Rajasthan.
Satyendra Nath Bose along with Nandalal Bose, Zakir Husain, Balasaheb Gangadhar Kher, V. K. Krishna Menon, and Jigme Dorji Wangchuck (Bhutan), who were honoured with Padma Vibhushan in 1954.

1957. Ramon Magsaysay Award is an annual award established to perpetuate former Philippine President Ramon Magsaysay's example of integrity in governance, courageous service to the people, and pragmatic idealism within a democratic society.

Mathematics. The prize is named after the Indian mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan. It was founded in 2004 and was first awarded in 2005. Another Award, SASTRA Ramanujan Prize was established in 2005. Q.1877. Which of the following organizations awards the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences? SSC CHSL 12/04/2021 (Evening) (a) Norwegian Nobel Committee (b) Karolinska Institute (c) The Royal Swedish Academy Sciences (d) Swedish Academy Sol.1877.(c) The Royal Swedish Academy Sciences. It was first awarded in to Dutch economist Jan Tinbergen and Norwegian economist Ragnar Frisch "for having developed and applied dynamic models for the analysis of economic processes.

Gobind Behari Lal. He was an Indian-American journalist and independence activist. He won the Pulitzer in in the journalism category. Pulitzer Prize - First awarded - 1917. Presented by - Columbia University. Awarded for - Excellence in newspaper journalism, literary achievements, musical composition.
Indian National Congress. This award was given for the first time in 1985. It is given on Indira Gandhi’s martyrdom day (31st October) and it carries an amount of 10 lakh in cash and a citation.
Barack Obama. He was awarded in 2009 for his “extraordinary efforts to strengthen international diplomacy and cooperation between people.”

Viswanathan Anand. He is an Indian chess grandmaster and also the former world champion. Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award (Dhyanchand Khel Ratna) is given for the spectacular and most outstanding performance in the field of sports by a sportsperson over a period of four years. The award was started in the year 1991-92.
Emil Jannings. The first Academy Awards ceremony was held in Hollywood on May 16, 1929.
